{{Quote|Quiet his voice was, dry his humor.|Yoda, regarding Vookto|<ref name="J:MW" />}}
'''Cei Vookto''' was a [[Duros/Legends|Duros]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] who served the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] and the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] in the [[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|Grand Army of the Republic]] during the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]]. He [[Jedi training/Legends|trained]] two [[Padawan/Legends|Padawans]], including the [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Dama Montalvo]]. [[22 BBY/Legends|Three months]] into the war, Vookto was dispatched to the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Lianna/Legends|Lianna]] to [[Ground warfare/Legends|defend]] a [[Sienar Fleet Systems/Legends|Sienar Fleet Systems]] [[Sienar Fleet Systems facility|starfighter facility]] from the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Separatists]]. He was [[Death/Legends|slain]] during the [[Battle of Lianna|ensuing battle]] when he sacrificed himself so that the [[Clone trooper/Legends|soldiers]] under his command could survive a Separatist attack. After the Republic won the battle, Vookto's body was given a [[Funeral of Cei Vookto|funeral pyre]] on Lianna, which was attended by Montalvo and several [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] Masters, including [[Yoda/Legends|Yoda]] and [[Mace Windu/Legends|Mace Windu]].

==Biography==
{{Quote|When the call came, Master Vookto returned immediately and took command of an army, without hesitation or question, and led ably.|Mace Windu|<ref name="J:MW" />}}
[[File:DamaMontalvo.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Dama Montalvo, Vookto's second Padawan]]
Cei Vookto, a [[Duros/Legends|Duros]] [[Gender/Legends|male]], was [[Jedi training/Legends|educated]] in the ways of [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] by the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]], eventually attaining the rank of [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]]. True to his heritage as a Duros, Vookto enjoyed traveling, embarking on long missions along the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]]. When he returned from his missions to the [[Jedi Temple/Legends|Jedi Temple]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]], he told stories of his adventures to any [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] who would listen. He eventually took an individual as his [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]] learner.<ref name="J:MW" />

By the [[Standard year/Legends|year]] [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]], after Vookto trained his first Padawan to [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Knighthood]], he selected the [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Dama Montalvo]] as his next apprentice. When the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]] between the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] and the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]] [[First Battle of Geonosis/Legends|erupted]] in that year, Vookto returned to Coruscant per the [[Jedi High Council/Legends|Jedi High Council's]] instructions and assumed a command position within the [[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|Grand Army of the Republic]].<ref name="J:MW" /> Three [[Standard month/Legends|months]] into the war,<ref name="NEC: TBLT" /> Vookto, Montalvo, and the [[Clone trooper/Legends|clone troopers]] under their command were dispatched to the planet [[Lianna/Legends|Lianna]]<ref name="J:MW" /> in order to [[Ground warfare/Legends|defend]] a [[Sienar Fleet Systems/Legends|Sienar Fleet Systems]] [[Sienar Fleet Systems facility|starfighter factory]]—which was essential for the Republic—from an attack by the Separatists.<ref name="J:MW" />

During the [[Battle of Lianna|ensuing battle]], Vookto sacrificed himself<ref name="J:MW" /> by using his [[Force power/Legends|abilities]] at summoning the elements, which caused a pillar of [[Fire/Legends|fire]] and a pillar of [[Water/Legends|water]] to cleanse the planet. Although Vookto perished due to his usage of the Force,<ref name="NEC: TBLT" /> his [[Squad/Legends|squadron]] of [[Soldier/Legends|soldiers]] survived an attack from the enemy.<ref name="CSWE">{{CSWECite|III|311|Vookto, Cei}}</ref> After the Republic secured a victory against the Separatists, Vookto was given a [[Funeral of Cei Vookto|funeral pyre]], which was attended by [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Masters]] [[Yoda/Legends|Yoda]], [[Mace Windu/Legends|Mace Windu]], [[Ki-Adi-Mundi/Legends|Ki-Adi-Mundi]], [[Tholme/Legends|Tholme]], and [[Quinlan Vos/Legends|Quinlan Vos]]. Also in attendance were Vookto's Padawan Montalvo—who was assigned to Ki-Adi-Mundi's command—and at least two of the [[Trooper/Legends|troopers]] who had been under the Duros Jedi's command.<ref name="J:MW" />

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|He sacrificed his own life today for his troops, who would have sacrificed their own for his. This was his victory.|Mace Windu, regarding Vookto|<ref name="J:MW" />}}
Cei Vookto was remembered by Master Yoda for having a quiet voice and a dry sense of humor. He also made it a point to tell Yoda and other Jedi tales of his travels. Vookto obeyed the [[Jedi Council/Legends|Council's]] decrees without hesitation, and took a commanding role in the Republic's Grand [[Army/Legends|Army]]. The Duros was a capable leader, and was willing to risk his life for the clone [[Troop/Legends|troopers]] under his command.<ref name="J:MW" />

==Powers and abilities==
Vookto was [[Force-sensitive/Legends|sensitive]] to the Force, and was capable enough to ascend to the rank of Jedi Master. He managed to successfully train one Padawan during his career, and tutored Dama Montalvo prior to perishing on Lianna.<ref name="J:MW" /> During the battle at Lianna, Vookto utilized his abilities at summoning the elements to cleanse the planet; however, the exertion from the effort eventually caused him to lose his life.<ref name="NEC: TBLT" />

==Behind the scenes==
Cei Vookto was first mentioned in the [[Star Wars comic books|comic book]] ''[[Jedi: Mace Windu]]'', which was released on [[February 26]], [[2003]] and written by [[John Ostrander]]. His role in the Battle of Lianna was expanded on in ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'', released in [[October 25]], [[2005]]. Vookto also gained an entry in [[2008]]'s ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''.
